ref: God will bless everyone who doesn't reject me because of what I do.”
text: Pʊpɛl sʊɔ lɛ a 'lɔ na ba baala ma ɛ.”
translated: The child is crying because he wants to eat.

ref: If you are tired from carrying heavy burdens, come to me and I will give you rest.
text: “Yɩ wa a n sɛ̃, yɩn banyã na bal na yɩ tuo tuor ka a wõnõ yɩ, ɩn too yɩn a yɩ pɛ̃n.
translated: When you are hungry, you will go and eat the food that is prepared for you, and you will be satisfied.

ref: The Pharisees got together and planned how they could trick Jesus into saying something wrong.
text: Be lɛ ka a Faraseemɩne guor taa na ba wa bɛɛr Yesu kaa na ba na nyɔɔ wʊ a wʊ 'yɛrbie pɔ.
translated: They said that the Pharisees were plotting against Jesus to kill him.

ref: Jesus walked on and soon saw James and John, the sons of Zebedee. They were in a boat, mending their nets.
text: Wʊn wa chen a niem bãlãa, wʊ nyɛ naa Jemesɩ nɩ Jɔɔn, Jebedee bibiir, a ba be a gboro pɔ chɔɔrɔ a lɔyaarsɩ.
translated: Once upon a time, there was a man named James and John, sons of Jebedee, who were fishermen.

ref: The seeds that fell on good ground are the people who hear and welcome the message. They produce
text: A anyã mɩ na lo a kɔjɔ̃ pɔ ɩ naa a bala na wõ a 'yɛr na ba sɔɔ de na ba dɔɔ jur, ka amɩne ɩ lɩjɛr nɩ pie, amɩne lɩjaata, amɩne mɩ kɔbaa.”
translated: The old woman said, "I will tell you a story about a man who went to the farm and saw a snake. The snake was very big and scary, but the man was not afraid. He killed the snake and took it home to eat."

ref: The angel said, “Get up and take the child and his mother back to Israel. The people who wanted to kill him are now dead.”
text: “Vaa ir 'mɔɔ a bie nɩ a wʊ ma na fʊ chiin a Isɩral wɛr pɔ, a bala na dɔ̃ɔ bʊɔrɔ a bie kʊfʊ kpi naa.”
translated: The man said that he went to a place called Israel where there is a lot of water, and he saw a big tree with many fruits.

ref: Everyone who asks will receive. Everyone who searches will find. And the door will be opened for everyone who knocks.
text: Nɩrɛ 'lɔ na sʊɔr na nyɛ naa, ka a 'lɔ mɩ na yɔbɔ nyɛ, ka a pãn mɩ yuo kʊ a nɩrɛ 'lɔ na kpoor.
translated: The child is playing with sand, and the mother is sitting down, and the father is going to the farm.

ref: Many prophets and good people were eager to see what you see and to hear what you hear. But I tell you they did not see or hear. Jesus Explains the Story about the Farmer (Mark
text: Yelmãɛ lɛ n 'yɛrɛ kʊrɔ yɩ ɩka Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛ'yɛrbɛ yɔɔ nɩ nɩtorsɩ yɔɔ ta bʊɔrɔ na ɩka ba nyɛ bon 'lɔ yɩn nyɛ tɩchɛ baa nyɛ ɛ, na ba ta wõ bon 'lɔ yɩn wõ tɩchɛ baa tʊ̃ɔ wõ ɛ.
translated: The woman said that she will go to the farm tomorrow, and she will go with her children. She said that she will go to the farm tomorrow, and she will go with her children.

ref: Everyone who has something will be given more, and they will have more than enough. But everything will be taken from those who don't have anything.
text: Nɩrɛ 'lɔ jaa na tara yɔɔ, ban kʊ wʊn yɔɔ pʊɔ ka ba tara yɔɔ tɩchɛ a nɩrɛ 'lɔ na ba tara bon jaa ɛ, a bãlãa 'lɔ wʊn tara gba ban faan yin a wʊ sɛ̃.
translated: When a person is going to farm, they will go and clear the bush before they start farming. And when a person is going to farm, they will go and clear the bush before they start farming. They will also go and clear the weeds before they start farming.

ref: So the chief priests met with the leaders and decided to bribe the soldiers with a lot of money.
text: A bɔɔrlo nãa nɩ a wʊ nɩbɛrɛ na wa lɔ̃ɔ taa, ba guor taa de libiyɔɔ kʊ a sojasɩ
translated: The old woman went to the farm to harvest her crops, but she saw a soldier.

ref: The Son of Man will send out his angels, and they will gather from his kingdom everyone who does wrong or causes others to sin.
text: A maa Nɩsaal Bie na tʊ̃n a n malakasɩ ka ba wa tuur a nɩsʊ̃n a nãalʊ̃ʊ pɔ, tɩchɛ a bala jaa na vɛ̃ ka a nɩbɛ maala yelbier nɩ yelbe-ɩrbɛ ba jaa 'mataa bar.
translated: The woman Nisaal Bie went to the farm to harvest cassava, but she saw a snake and ran away quickly, fearing it might bite her.

ref: Jesus told the man to stand up where everyone could see him.
text: Yesu 'yɛr kʊ a daba nũpɛl na kpi, “Ir ara a sɩ jaa nyɛ fʊ.”
translated: Jesus said to him, "Today salvation has come to this house."

ref: So the words of the prophet Jeremiah came true, “They took the thirty silver coins, the price of a person among the people of Israel.
text: A ɩn yelmãɛ nɩ a lɛ a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛ'yɛrɛ Jeremiya na dɔ̃ɔ 'yɛr, “Ba den a libibie lɩjɛr nɩ pie a lɛ a Isɩral nɩbɛ na sɔɔ de,
translated: The Lord said to Jeremiah, “Go and proclaim these words to the people of Israel.

ref: Everyone will hate you because of me. But if you remain faithful until the end, you will be saved.
text: Nɩrɛ jaa na 'laar yɩ naa yɩn sɔɔ ma jũu, tɩchɛ nɩrɛ 'lɔ na sɔɔ ara tɩ ta a baaraa, na nyɛn faafʊ.
translated: The child is crying because he wants to eat, but there is no food in the house, so we are all hungry.

ref: Some Pharisees noticed this and said to Jesus, “Why are your disciples picking grain on the Sabbath? They are not supposed to do this!”
text: A Faraseemɩne na wa nyɛ a lɛ, ba sʊʊr a Yesu, “Fʊ nyɛn lɛ na ba tu a nɛbinãa a fʊ poturbo na ɩrɛ a Pɛ̃nfʊ Bɩbir Daar ɔ?”
translated: And they said to him, "Who are you, that you should say these things? What authority do you have to do these things?"

ref: But the chief priests and the leaders convinced the crowds to ask for Barabbas to be set free and for Jesus to be killed.
text: Tɩchɛ a bɔɔrlo nɩbɛrɛ nɩ a nɩbɛrɛ 'yɛr kʊ a nɩyɔɔ ɩka ba sʊɔr ka wʊ yuo Barabasɩ bar, tɩ kʊ a Yesu.
translated: They were talking about the one who was going to be crucified, that he was going to be released instead of Jesus.

ref: “What do you think about the Messiah? Whose family will he come from?” They answered, “He will be a son of King David.”
text: “Ŋmɩnɛ lɛ yɩ tɩɛr a Nɩ-iraa yele? An bie lɛ wʊ ɩ?” Ka ba 'yɛr kʊ wʊ, “David bie lɛ.”
translated: Who is the one who went to Niraa yesterday? And who is he? They said, "David is the one."

ref: Jesus answered, “Not one of you knows what you are asking. Are you able to drink from the cup that I must soon drink from?” James and John said, “Yes, we are!”
text: Ka a Yesu 'yɛr, “Faa bɔ̃ɔ a bon 'lɔ fʊn sʊɔrɔ ɛ. Ban tʊ̃ɔ nyũn a dɔɔyɛ ŋmele 'lɔ ɩn na nyũn ɔ?” Ka ba 'yɛr, “Sɩn tʊ̃ɔ naa.”
translated: And Jesus said, "Who touched me?" And they all denied.

ref: We could have sold this perfume for a lot of money and given it to the poor.”
text: Fʊn taa den a kãnyũu anyãna da libiyɔɔ de kʊ a nɔ̃n dẽme.”
translated: The man went to the farm to cut down a tree for firewood.

ref: He even had to tell his disciples to get a boat ready to keep him from being crushed by the crowds.
text: A nɩyɔɔ jũu, Yesu 'yɛr kʊ a wʊ poturbo, “Yɩ kaa nyɛ gboro kʊ ma ka n sɛr bãlãa.” Wʊ 'yɛr naa a lɛ bojũu waa bʊɔrɔ ɩka a nɩbɛ ba mɩl pɔɔ wʊ ɛ.
translated: When Jesus saw this, he said, “It is not the healthy who need a doctor, but the sick.”
